It seems some of you dont look at history vary much. the FLSA, was made back when the price was labor was not regulated, This means that people where being paid WELL below what was acceptable for the work they were doing. when it was first set up in 10/24/1938 it was .25 per hour. Now for those of you do not know what growth and inflation means,  That had the same buying power as someone earning 23 dollars today.  If you wish to check the math please be my guest. The work places who set the pay felt they could charge what ever they wanted for their labor and chose to label it as 'unskilled work' to try and hide their practice of under paying their employees. Sadly this idea stuck in the mind of alot of people that 'unskilled' means worthless or not impactful.  fast food, hard labor,  driving, janitors and even waste management  was called unskilled labor. so they could under pay those they hired, FDR, saw through this as did many people in the government and saw how much this was hurting the American people.  The FLSA and many other bills are all all helped create what we know as middle cast Americans. around the 70's or 80's is when we start to see the government start to become lobbied by these companies about the min wage and we start to see a drop in the growth of the in wage untill it sits where it was between 2000-2007. 

with this being said you can call me Delusional all you want. but in the end of the day, its not the people asking for the ability to pay their bills thats the problem. Look at these companies who report record braking profits year after year. not even 30% of that comes back in to the pockets of the state or to the pockets of the people. and the point of that statment is "profit" this is the amount left over after all costs and employees are paid. 

The problem is we have spent years fighting for fair pay in this world and  it has been on the back slide since after nixon. 

but its fine call people names rather then look it up with your own eyes. do your own leg work before you tell people they are wrong.

author: @GratefullyDeadRiskManager
text: This is starting to happen even in non entry level jobs. I work as a debt resolution specialty advisor and my company has used AI for years to scan and score our calls, flag section of calls….. the AI basically does the same job that I did for years as a Quality Analyst except that it doesn’t make human judgement calls. And it can score and scan thousands of calls instantly instead of one at a time. And it’s never been good and was already dystopian. But my first question was, why wouldn’t the AI be scanning all these calls with the ultimate intent to learn how to just do all of the human jobs. I really think that’s the long term goal. And this is a $40-$50 an hour job that I have over a decade of experience doing. It’s going to gut entry level jobs. But it’s also going to hit any type of help desk, tech support, data analysis, quality analyst jobs even that aren’t exactly entry level.
